# Phase 1 — Technical Plan: Project Setup & Database
**Spec Kit artifact** · Generated by `/speckit.plan` based on spec.md

---

## Architecture Decisions

| Decision | Choice | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| Runtime | Node.js 20 LTS | Stability, LTS support |
| Framework | Express 4 | Minimal, well-understood |
| DB driver | `pg` (node-postgres) | Direct SQL control, no ORM overhead |
| DB pool | `pg.Pool` with max 10 connections | Handles concurrent requests safely |
| Config | `dotenv` + startup validator | Fails fast on missing config |

---

## Folder Structure

```
backend/
├── config/
│   └── db.js            ← pg.Pool instance + connection test
├── database/
│   ├── schema.sql        ← all CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS statements
│   ├── seed.sql          ← INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
│   ├── migrate.js        ← reads schema.sql, executes against DB
│   └── seed.js           ← reads seed.sql, executes against DB
├── middleware/
│   └── errorHandler.js   ← global Express error handler
├── routes/
│   └── health.js         ← GET /health
├── .env.example
├── package.json
└── server.js             ← app bootstrap
```

---

## server.js Bootstrap Order

```
1. Load dotenv
2. validateEnv() — check all required vars
3. Create Express app
4. Mount middleware (helmet → cors → json → cookieParser → morgan)
5. Mount routes (/health)
6. Mount global error handler (last)
7. testDbConnection() → if fails, process.exit(1)
8. app.listen(PORT)
```

---

## Environment Variables Required

```
PORT, NODE_ENV,
DB_HOST, DB_PORT, DB_NAME, DB_USER, DB_PASSWORD,
JWT_SECRET, JWT_EXPIRES_IN,
STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET,
SENDGRID_API_KEY, EMAIL_FROM, EMAIL_FROM_NAME,
FRONTEND_URL
```

---

## Schema Summary

```sql
users           (id, first_name, last_name, email, password, phone,
                 organization, country, city, role, created_at, updated_at)

plans           (id, key, name, price, description, is_active, created_at)

orders          (id, order_number, user_id→users, plan_id→plans, quantity,
                 unit_price, promo_code, discount_amount, fee_amount,
                 total_amount, currency, status, payment_method,
                 stripe_payment_id, created_at)

tickets         (id, order_id→orders, user_id→users, ticket_code,
                 plan_key, is_used, used_at, created_at)

promo_codes     (id, code, discount_type, discount_value, max_uses,
                 used_count, expires_at, is_active, created_at)

speakers        (id, name, role, bio, talk_title, talk_desc, image_url,
                 order_index, is_active, created_at)

sponsors        (id, name, logo_url, website, tier, is_active, created_at)

host_applications (id, user_id→users, organization, venue_name,
                   venue_capacity, city, country, message,
                   status, submitted_at)

inquiries       (id, name, email, type, message, created_at)
```

---

## Key Indexes

```sql
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX ON users(email);
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX ON orders(order_number);
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX ON tickets(ticket_code);
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX ON promo_codes(code);
CREATE INDEX ON orders(user_id);
CREATE INDEX ON orders(status);
CREATE INDEX ON tickets(order_id);
```

---

## Response Shape Contract

All API responses (established in this phase, used everywhere):

```json
// Success
{ "success": true, "data": { ... } }

// Error
{ "success": false, "error": "Human-readable message", "details": [...] }
```

---

## Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| DB connection fails silently | `testDbConnection()` on startup with `process.exit(1)` |
| Schema drift between environments | `schema.sql` is the single source — run migrate on every deploy |
| Seed runs twice creates duplicates | `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` on all seed rows |
| Missing env var breaks runtime | `validateEnv()` checks all keys before app starts |
